rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JOHN DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner John Does Petition To Quash Out-Of-State Administrative Subpoena Request Form To Google Llc Or Alternatively For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: November 12, 2019 Line 1, PETITIONER JOHN DOE'S Petition To Quash Out-Of-State Administrative Subpoena Request Form To Google Llc Or Alternatively For A Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to Nov-19-2019 at 9:00 a.m. Judge Pro Tem Scott Borrowman recuses himself. (email dated 10-28-19) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302) [Unopposed - don't have papers]

rulings.Case Title: ANNE MAUREEN DOWLING VS. UNITED AIRLINES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issuance Of A Request For Judicial Assistance (Letters Rogatory) Under The Hague Convention
rulings.Rulings: November 12, 2019 Line 9 1), DEFENDANT UNITED AIRLINES, INC. 'S Motion For Issuance Of A Request For Judicial Assistance (Letters Rogatory) Under The Hague Convention The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED. The Hague Convention and implementing statutes permit the request for judicial assistance sought. 23 UST 2555, 28 U.S.C. 1781; Societe Nationale Industrielle Aerospatiale v. U.S. Dist. Ct. for S. Dist. of Iowa, 482 U.S. 522, 535 (1987). The Opposition focuses on the delay in seeking the letters of request, but this does not demonstrate why the request should not be granted. Even if the Court were to find a delay, that would not preclude the letters of request because discovery is still open. Moving party must provide an updated proposed letter of request. The current one refers to Santa Clara County ( 5), the trial date is no longer accurate ( 12), and the signature block refers to Los Angeles County. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302)

rulings.Case Title: DEVLIN/MCNALLEY CONSTRUCTION, INC. VS. MISSION HUB, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice Of Motion And Devlinmcnally Construction, Inc.S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 12, 2019, Line 8, PLAINTIFF DEVLIN/MCNALLEY CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication Plaintiff Devlin/McNally Construction Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff failed to maintain its initial burden of production. To prevail on its foreclosure of mechanic's lien claim, Plaintiff is obligated to show the reasonable value of its work/services, because the amount due is the lesser of the reasonable value of the work provided by the claimant and the contractually agreed price. (Civ. Code § 8430(a)(1).) "The actual amount due on the lien presents a question of fact for the trial court." (Basic Modular Facilities, Inc. v. Ehsanipour (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 1480, 1485.) In this case, Plaintiff has not shown by undisputed evidence the reasonable value of its services, and it is even unclear what amount of damages Plaintiff is seeking to recover. Plaintiff's points and authorities appear to argue that $520,270.00 (the amount of the original recorded lien) is owed. Plaintiff's UMF 9 states that Mission HUB LLC failed to pay "nearly $500,000.00" for the services. Plaintiff's UMF 24 refers to the $348,849.52 judgment that Plaintiff obtained against Mission HUB LLC. UMF 29 states that Plaintiff made improvements "resulting in approximately a twenty-five percent or two and a half million dollar increase in the property's value." The court cannot grant summary adjudication based upon such uncertain and varying approximations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to submit a proposed order for signature and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: TAMMY TRAVERSO VS. PACIFIC MARITIME ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Pacific Maritime Association
rulings.Rulings: November 12, 2019 Line 6, PLAINTIFF TAMMY TRAVERSO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Pacific Maritime Association The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. At hearing, Plaintiff shall be prepared to identify what she means by "statistical data." Re timing: Plaintiff shall be prepared to discuss why information from years she was not eligible for promotion consideration is relevant and discoverable. Re geographic scope: the parties shall be prepared to address what decisionmaking functions regarding walking boss promotions are done outside the Port of Stockton, including any situations where the Steering Committee did not adopt a recommendation from the specific port. Re "complaints": because "complaints" or "negative feedback" could mean anything from standard workplace griping about a coworker to contacting HR or filing a grievance, there must be some meaningful way for Defendant to identify what to search. Plaintiff shall be prepared to address what information - other than grievances under the CBA - may be responsive. Similarly, "coaching" could refer to casual advice to formal performance processes. Plaintiff must define this. Defendant must address the search for pre-complaint demand letters and the like referenced in the motion. Re other personnel: Plaintiff shall be prepared to address why she needs information on "each and every Class B registered worker" or Walking Boss, as this does not appear calculated to lead to admissible evidence on her claims. Re the selectee's full personnel file: If only 5 years worth of records was considered for any applicant, Plaintiff shall be prepared to address why discovery of more than 5 years is proper or relevant. It appears the parties have meet and conferred to limit this RFP but it is not clear how limited. For example, insurance and retirement information would not be relevant, but some information about alleged sloppy work practices would. Re damage caused by longshore workers: Defendant shall be prepared to address purported burden. The parties shall be prepared to discuss the status of discussions about Form Interrogatory 15.1. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302)

The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off calendar per the request and agreement of all parties. (email 11-4-19) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302)

rulings.Case Title: PREMISE DATA CORPORATION A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. STEFFEN WEISS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: November 8, 2019 Line 8, DEFENDANT STEFFEN WEISS' Motion For Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion DENIED. The Court is receptive to Defendant's statements of financial difficulty and the parties are free to meet-and-confer to identify possible alternatives to streamlining discovery on the evidence on which the cross-complaint is based. However, there is no basis for compelling such a result because the special interrogatories are classic contention interrogatories permitted by CCP 2030.010(b); Singer v. Sup. Ct. (1960) 54 Cal.2d 318, 324 (permitting contention interrogatories). As Plaintiff notes, Rifkind v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 1255, 1262-63, states a preference for interrogatories over deposition questions to discover the bases of allegations in a complaint. Form Interrogatory 15.1 permits an interrogatory to require identification of facts, witnesses, and documents in support of each denial or affirmative defense. The form interrogatories do not have a parallel request for information that supports allegations in a complaint, but that there are form interrogatories for this type of discovery underscores that it is permitted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302)
